name: mariqt

metadata.name: mariqt
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: The MareHub & Marine Imaging Community Image QA/QC and curation toolbox

documentation: We strive to make marine image data FAIR. We develop FDOs for images to establish a common language for marine imagery, we develop best-practice operating procedures for handling marine images and we develop the MarIQT software to apply the iFDOs and procedures to marine imagery. MarIQT stands for Image Quality control / quality assurance and curation Tools (IQT) conceptualised and developed by the MareHub working group on Videos/Images (part of the DataHub, a research data manangement initiative by the Helmholtz association).

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.__init__
documentation:
Creates a provenance object

Creates a new provenance object with or without catching up on a provenance history,
depending on whether paths to valid previous provenance files are given
Provide the arguments in a dict like so: ['name':...,'value':...[,'hash':...]] where the
hash is optional but required for files. If a file path is given without a hash
this function will compute it.
verbose: if True all logs are also printed
tmpFilePath: if provided all logs will be immediately written to a temporary file which will be deleted once process finished properly

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.addPreviousProvenance
documentation:
Adds a file's content as a previous provenance information.

The file dost not have to contain a valid provenance yaml format.
In case it does, this information is copied.
In case it does not, only the log block will be populated.

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.log
documentation: Add a text string to the log and eventually show (i.e. print) it.

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.addArgument
documentation: Adds one argument to the provenance arguments list

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.addUsedSettings
documentation: Adds all used settings from a settings object to the provenance arguments.

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.write
documentation:
Write the provenance info to disk.

The provenance file is created in the folder given by path and named:
<name>_provenance-<executable>-<datetime>.yaml

methods.name: mariqt.provenance.Provenance.write2TmpLogFile
documentation: writes to temporary log file in case program exits before properly calling write()

functions.name: mariqt.equipment.equipmentOwner
documentation: Returns the owner part of an equipment id (everything until the first underscore)

functions.name: mariqt.equipment.equipmentType
documentation: Returns the type part of an equipment id (between the first underscore and the first dash)

functions.name: mariqt.equipment.equipmentShortName
documentation: Returns the unique short version of the id (owner_type-index)

functions.name: mariqt.equipment.equipment_url
documentation:
returns constructed equipment git url to https://dm.pages.geomar.de/equipment/...
Reachability of url should be checked.

modules.name: mariqt.reports
documentation: This files contains various functions to create HTML reports about data

functions.name: mariqt.reports.createReport
documentation: Creates the entire report HTML and stores it at path

functions.name: mariqt.image.getVideoRuntime
documentation: Uses FFMPEG to determine the runtime of a video in seconds. Returns 0 in case their was any issue

functions.name: mariqt.image.getVideoStartTime
documentation: Uses FFMPEG to determine the start time of a video from the metadata of the video file. Returns an empty string in case there was any issue

functions.name: mariqt.image.getVideoUUID
documentation:
For multiple photos per directory getImageUUIDsForFolder() is much faster.
Uses exiftool to query an video file for a UUID in its metadata (-identifier) or for mkv uses mkvinfo and 'Segment UID'. 
Returns tuple parsed_uuid, completeMessage. parsed_uuid is an empty string if there is no UUID encoded in the file.

functions.name: mariqt.image.getPhotoUUID
documentation:
For multiple files per directory getImageUUIDsForFolder() is much faster.
        uses exiftool to query an image file for a UUID in its metadata 0xa420 ImageUniqueID (-exif:imageuniqueid, unless strict == False) 
        Returns tuple parsed_uuid, completeMessage. parsed_uuid is an empty string if there is no UUID encoded in the file.
Params:
        strict: if False uses -imageuniqueid which also allows for canon:imageuniqueid (0x0028  ImageUniqueID) which is currently 
        NOT SUPPORTED by Elements!

functions.name: mariqt.image.imageContainsValidUUID
documentation:
returns whether image contains valid UUID and UUID. Throws exception if file extension not in variables.photo_types or variables.video_types
For many files per directory use the faster imagesContainsValidUUID()

functions.name: mariqt.image.imagesContainValidUUID
documentation: retruns dict {'file':{'uuid':str,'valid:bool'}}

functions.name: mariqt.image.getImagesExifFieldValuesForFolder
documentation: Uses exiftool to query all images in folder path for the field value in their metadata (is much faster then doing it for each file separately). Returns a dict with filename -> UUID keys/values

functions.name: mariqt.image.getImagesAllExifValues
documentation: Uses exiftool to query all images in folder path for all their exif metadata (is much faster then doing it for each file separately). Returns a dict with filename -> exif keys/values

functions.name: mariqt.image.getImagesAllExifValuesForFolder
documentation: Uses exiftool to query all images in folder path for all their exif metadata (is much faster then doing it for each file separately). Returns a dict with filename -> exif keys/values

functions.name: mariqt.image.getImageUUIDsForFolder
documentation:
Uses exiftool to query all images in folder path for the UUID in their metadata (is much faster then doing it for each file separately). 
Returns a dict with filename -> UUID keys/values.

functions.name: mariqt.image.browseForImageFiles
documentation:
Recursively scans a folder for media files (specified by the given file extension you are looking for).

The result variable contains a dictionary with the found file paths as keys and
a triple for each of those files with the file size, runtime and file extension:
<name>:[<size>,<runtime>,<ext>]
sub_folder_ignore: list of strings containing names of folders which are to be ignored while
scanning folder for image data

functions.name: mariqt.image.getVideosRuntime_multiThread
documentation: calls getVideoRuntime() in separate threads to speed up the process

functions.name: mariqt.image.createImageList
documentation:
Creates a text file that contains one line per image file found in path

Can overwrite an existing file list file if told so.
Can add the full absolute path to the text file if told so (by providing the absolute path you want as the write_path variable)
Can filter which images (or actually all file types) to put into the file. Default is all image types.

functions.name: mariqt.image.computeImageScaling
documentation:
Turns an ASCII file with image->area information into an ASCII file with image->scaling information

Path to the source file is given, path to the result file can be given or is constructed from the convention

functions.name: mariqt.image.createImageItemsDictFromImageItemsList
documentation: Creates from a list of item dicts a dict of dicts with the 'image-filename' value becoming the respective dicts name

functions.name: mariqt.image.createImageItemsListFromImageItemsDict
documentation: Creates from a dict of item dicts a list of dicts with the 'image-filename' value becoming an item field again

functions.name: mariqt.image.parseImageFileName
documentation:
Parses info from file name according to convetion: <event>_<sensor>_<date>_<time>.<ext> 
Returns event, sensor or "","" in case of invalid equipment type (see variables.equipment_types).

functions.name: mariqt.image.checkFilesUUIDsInDir
documentation:
Checks UUIDs of files in path (sud its subdirs if recursive==True).
Retruns lists files_recognized, files_no_uuid_supported,files_no_uuid_unsupported

functions.name: mariqt.image.writeUUIDtoPhoto
documentation:
Uses exiftool to write UUID to copy of photo. Original will be saved as path+'_original'.
Caution: check if file contains an uuid already, otherwise its overwritten which should not happen.
Returns success, new name of original file

functions.name: mariqt.image.writeUUIDtoVideo
documentation:
Uses XMP Toolkit to write UUID to copy of file in XMP namespace Dublin Core metadata field 'identifier'. 
Original will be saved as path+'_original'.
Caution: check if file contains an uuid already, otherwise its overwritten which should not happen. 
Returns success, new name of original file

functions.name: mariqt.sources.osis.get_expedition_ids

returns dict {cruise_name: cruise_id} of all expeditions. 
RATHER use get_expedition_id_from_label() if you look for a single
expeditions id - much faster

functions.name: mariqt.sources.osis.get_expedition_id_from_label

queries osis for the expedition label and returns its id if exact match was found, 
otherwise raise ValueError.

functions.name: mariqt.sources.osis.get_expeditions_from_label

functions.name: mariqt.sources.osis.get_expedition_id_from_url

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.addEndToDSHIPEventsByLastAction

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.renameEvents

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.unzipDSHIPExport
documentation: Unzips a dship export zip file and distributes the files to the proper folder.

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.findDSHIPSourceFile
documentation: Searches for a file in the sensor part of the path that has the string '.dat' in it

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.parseDSHIPDeviceOperationsOrEventsFile
documentation: Tries to read a DSHIP file from disk. Can differentiate between deviceoperations and events files

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.parseDSHIPEventsFile
documentation: Parse a DSHIP export file for events

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.parseDSHIPDeviceOperationsFile
documentation: Parse a DSHIP export file for device operations

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.exportNavigationFromDSHIPForOneStation
documentation: A function to call for each event to perform the HTTP request to the DSHIP database. Start and end times need to be UTC string in the format: %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S%z

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.exportNavigationDataFromDSHIP
documentation:
Calls the DSHIP API to extract information

This function takes a list of events in the form:
        [{'name':<station_name>,
        'start':<station start time (timestamp)>,
        'end':<station end time (timestamp)>},
        ...[more stations]]
and executes calls to the DSHIP API to fetch data for those events (aka stations).
If the event list contains beacon ids ('beacon_id':<usbl beacon id - optional>) in the range >= 0
it will request USBL data, otherwise it will fetch the ship's navigation data.
Requires a user name and user email under which the data will be made available on the DSHIP server.
You can set the data frequency (in seconds) at which USBL data points are delivered and the
usbl_beacon_id for which data shall be exported. This is a general parameter for all
request but can be overwritten by specifying a beacon_id in the events list.

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.parseStationInfoFromDSHIPActionLogFile
documentation: returns a dict: {<station>: {'start':<datetime>,'end':<datetime>}}

functions.name: mariqt.sources.dship.removeEmptyLines
documentation: removes empty lines from file, returnes cleaned file path

functions.name: mariqt.sources.ofop.findOFOPDataFiles
documentation: Find OFOP annotation files in a folder

functions.name: mariqt.sources.ofop.getOFOPAnnotations
documentation:
Loads all annotations from either an obser or prot file

Type can be "obser" or "prot"
Multi-row replacements handle subsequent annotations belonging to the same object:
they are defined by mrr['pre'] = first label, mrr['cur'] = second label, mrr['rep'] = replacement of first label.
The second annotation will be ignored and thus its label not returned!

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.getMinMaxLatLon
documentation:
Returns the minimum and maximum latitude and longitude values of the given positions.

Returns 0,0,0,0 if the positions are empty

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.readAllPositionsFromFilePath
documentation:
Load all navigation data from file.

Does so based on the file path, the name of the pos_header to extract (see variables.py)
and the date_format string.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.readAllPositionsFromFile
documentation: Read all positions and store by timestamp

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.readAllAttitudesFromFilePath
documentation:
Load all attitude data from file.

Does so based on the file path, the name of the pos_header to extract (see variables.py)
and the date_format string.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.readAllAttitudesFromFile
documentation: Read all Attiudes and store by timestamp

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.runAutomatedCuration
documentation: Runs all steps required for an automated curation of the navigation data.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.removeDuplicatePositions
documentation:
Deletes positions where the lat, lon (and dep if depthSeparate==False) values are identical for more then maxIdenticalSecs consecutive time points.
If setNone==True not the whole position is deleted but the respective value, in case also hgt, is set to None.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.removeDuplicateAttitudes
documentation: Deletes attitudes where the yaw, pitch and roll values are identical for more then maxIdenticalSecs consecutive time points.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.removeTemporalOutliers
documentation: Remove temporal outliers

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.removeSpeedOutliers
documentation: Removes position outliers by checking for values exceeding a given lateral and vertical speed threshold Speeds given im [m/s], time gap given in [s]

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.removeSpatialOutliers
documentation: Removes positions that have few close neighbors (potential outliers). Distances in meters, time_window_size in seconds

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.getPositionsInTimeRange
documentation: returns list of position within time range. Start search at t_start. Postions Must be ordered by time

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.smoothPositionsSlidingGaussian
documentation:
Smoothes position data with a sliding Gaussian window.

Takes raw position data and applies a sliding window smoothing using a Gaussian
to adjust the impact of data values to a specific time point by their temporal
distance from said time point. You can adjust the size of the gaussian by the
gauss_half_width paramater in seconds. The whole gaussian window is of size 2*gauss_half_width+1

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.smoothPositionsSliding2dMedianLatLonGaussianDepth
documentation:
Smoothes lat lon position data with a sliding geometric median and depth with a sliding Gaussian window.

Takes raw position data and applies a sliding window smoothing using a geometric median for lat/lon and a Gaussian for the depth
to adjust the impact of data values to a specific time point by their temporal
distance from said time point. You can adjust the size of the windows by the
<..>_half_width paramaters in seconds. The whole windows are of size 2*<..>_half_width+1

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.splineToOneSecondInterval
documentation: Fills gaps bigger one sec in the position list with 1 Hz interpolated samples

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.selectSamplingPosition
documentation: Selects the sampling location from a position profile as the the maximum depth time point

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.selectTransectMiddlePoint
documentation: Selects the position of the median time point of a series

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.checkPositionContent
documentation:
Check whether all nav data of one col are equal or empty.

Returns seven booleans: lat_identical,lon_identical,dep_identical,hgt_identical,dep_not_zero,hgt_not_zero,uncert_not_zero.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.checkPositionProductsExists
documentation: Check whether all the products exist as files in the given path

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writeMultiplePositionTypes
documentation: Can produce several position output formats at once.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositions
documentation: Write positions any kind of file, specified by the output_type. Optionally also attitudes can be written. They must have the same utc timestamps as the positions and will have the default headers.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositionsToTrack
documentation: Type 'track' has no header, it just dumps the positions consecutively without time information

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositionsToGxTrack
documentation: Type 'gx_track' has no header, it just dumps the time points consecutively followed by the positions

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositionsToKML
documentation: Type 'kml' adds an XML header to a gx_track data representation, including a name

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositionsToGeoJSON
documentation: Type 'geojson' turns the positions into a json representation. It is recommened to use this format!

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.writePositionsToTabFile
documentation:
Write a tab separated ascii file of the positions. You can specify which column headers shall be used for the various output types defined in definitions.py
Optionally also attitudes can be written. They must have the same utc timestamps as the positions and will have the default headers.

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.getSmoothedPositionAtMaxDepth
documentation: returns the smoothed position at max depth, max depth, max depth time

functions.name: mariqt.navigation.getSmoothedPositionAtDateTime
documentation: returns median position at time point

functions.name: mariqt.geo.interpolateAngles
documentation: interpolates angle at t_target between to angles x_pre and x_post, with respective times t_pre and t_post

functions.name: mariqt.geo.findNearestNeighbors
documentation: returns prev_nearest_index, past_nearest_index of value in values starting the search at startIndex. Throws exception if value out of range
TODO move to core?

functions.name: mariqt.geo.distanceLatLon
documentation: Computes a distance in meters from two given decimal lat/lon values.

functions.name: mariqt.geo.distancePositions
documentation: Computes a distance in meters from two given positions

functions.name: mariqt.geo.getDecDegCoordinate
documentation: Asserts that the given value is a decimal degree float value

functions.name: mariqt.geo.decdeg2decmin
documentation: Turns a float representation of a coordinate (lat/lon) into a string representation using decimal minutes

functions.name: mariqt.geo.decmin2decdeg
documentation: Converts a decimal degree string ("117° 02.716' W") to a decimal degree float

functions.name: mariqt.geo.Rx
documentation: Rotation matrix around x, take angle in degrees

functions.name: mariqt.geo.Ry
documentation: Rotation matrix around y, take angle in degrees

functions.name: mariqt.geo.Rz
documentation: Rotation matrix around z, take angle in degrees

functions.name: mariqt.geo.R_YawPitchRoll
documentation: return yaw pitch roll rotation matrix, takes angle in degrees

functions.name: mariqt.geo.R_XYZ

functions.name: mariqt.geo.yawPitchRoll
documentation: retruns [yaw,pitch,roll] in degrees from yaw,pitch,roll rotation matrix

functions.name: mariqt.geo.addLeverarms2LatLonDepAlt
documentation:
Adds and offest in vehicle coordinates to an original lat, lon, depth(downward positive), altitude position (wgs84)
and returns new lat, lon, depth, alt. altitude has no influence on results and can be set to anything/ignored if not needed. 
Angles in degrees, offsets in meters

functions.name: mariqt.geo.writeSimpleUtmWorldFile
documentation:
Writes a simple world file with UTM coordinates assuming the camera looks straight down, the principal point is in the center of the image and quadratic pixels
* altitude in meters
* focal length in pixels
* heading in degrees

functions.name: mariqt.geo.median2D

A class defining a 4.5D position, encoded by a utc time (unix in milliseconds), latitude, longitude, depth, height and coordinate uncertainty. Depth, height and coordinate uncertainty are optional.

Check whether all nav data of one col are equal or empty.

classes.name: mariqt.geo.AngularInterpolater

methods.name: mariqt.geo.AngularInterpolater.__init__
documentation:
Allows for interpolation of angular values via .interpolateAt(). source_t must be list of floats. 
Angular quantities can not be simply interpolated (e.g. middle of 175 and -175 would be zero but should be 180).
This class handles such cases properly

Allows for interpolation of angular values via .interpolateAt(). source_t must be list of floats. 
Angular quantities can not be simply interpolated (e.g. middle of 175 and -175 would be zero but should be 180).
This class handles such cases properly

documentation:
Takes an iFDO and sends its image coordinates to the OSIS underway database

Does no sanity checks at all. Currently only works for images.

functions.name: mariqt.sources.underway.uploadEventListToUnderway

classes.name: mariqt.sources.underway.MarIQTConnector
documentation:
Connects the mariqt positions world to the OSIS underway positions world.

Create an instance of this class and provide it with the API URL (ask cfaber for one if you do not know it) and
a platform (shortname) for the gear you are adding positions for (again, ask cfaber ... ).
Then get your positions ready in a mariqt.geo.Positions format.
If you want to add payload to the data (underway-speech for e.g. parameters like temperature at a position,
or a station name) then you also need to provide this as a list of equal size as the Positions list.
Once you have the instance of this object created, run its *do_import* method to do the magic!

functions.name: mariqt.misc.iFDOFieldDefinitionFromMarkdownTableToDictStr
documentation: DEPRECATED! reads field defintions from tables in markdown files and return str to create dict in variables.py

modules.name: mariqt.directories
documentation: This class implements the MareHub AGVI directory structure convention. this convention specifies that data should be structured like so: /base/project/[Gear/]event/sensor/data_type/ E.g.: /mnt/nfs/cruises/SO268/SO268-1_021-1_OFOS-02/SONNE_CAM-01_OFOS-Still/raw/

functions.name: mariqt.directories.findBaseForDir
documentation: Checks whether the required dir exists in any of the base_paths

functions.name: mariqt.directories.findSensorInEvents
documentation: Returns the absolute paths to all sensor directories that contain search in their name

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.__init__
documentation:
Requires a base_dir and the subsequent part dir of the directory path. If base_dir = "" dir is split in base_dir and dir automatically assuming dir points to TYPE folder. Will create a missing directory path if asked to do so.
(e.g. base_dir = "/mnt/nfs/cruises/", dir = "SO268/SO268-1_021-1_OFOS-02/SONNE_CAM-01_OFOS-Still/raw").

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.dump
documentation: Dumps the directory path to the console, mainly for debugging. Use str() instead to get a proper directory string

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.str
documentation: Turns the Dir object information into a directory string. Returns everything of the directory path that is known.

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.validDataDir
documentation: Validates whether directory information is available until the data_type

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.exists
documentation: Checks whether the directory of this object exists

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.create
documentation: Creates the directory of this object if it does not exist

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.replace
documentation: Can replace one (!) part of the object's directory (given by the keyword in dir) and replaces it with the value in new_val. Useful to change e.g. from one sensor to another or from one event to another. This only returns a string! In case you want to have another Dir object, use replaceCreateDir() instead.

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.replaceCreateDir
documentation: Can replace one (!) part of the object's directory (given by the keyword in dir) and replaces it with the value in new_val. Useful to change e.g. from one sensor to another or from one event to another. This returns a new Dir object! In case you only want to have a string, use replace() instead.

methods.name: mariqt.directories.Dir.createTypeFolder
documentation: Creates all the type folders

functions.name: mariqt.core.parseFileDateTimeAsUTC
documentation:
Parses datetime from from file name according to file naming convention <event>_<sensor>_<date>_<time>.<ext>
with date fromat yyyymmdd and time format HHMMSS[.f] with [] being optional. Throws exception if unsuccessfull

functions.name: mariqt.core.recursivelyUpdateDicts
documentation:
Recursively updates oldDict with newDict.
Merges item entries with identical image-datetime.
Item entries without image-datetime will get image default (start) datetime.
Item lists will be sorted by image-datetime.

functions.name: mariqt.core.recursivelyRemoveEmptyFields

modules.name: mariqt.tests
documentation: This file contains various validation functions to check whether given parameters adhere to the MareHub AG V/I conventions

functions.name: mariqt.tests.validate_ifdo
documentation:
Validate ifdo against schema 
Returns valid:bool, message:str
def isValidPerson(o:dict,msg=[]):
""" Checks whether the dict contains the fields needed to identify a person"""
if not isinstance(o,dict):
msg.append("person is not a dict")
return False
req = miqtv.req_person_fields
for r in req:
if not r in o or o[r] == "":
continue
if r == "email" and not isValidEmail(o[r]):
msg.append("invalid email")
return False
if r == "orcid" and not isValidOrcid(o[r]):
msg.append("invalid orcid")
return False
return True

functions.name: mariqt.tests.validate_against_schema
documentation:
validate ifdo aginst json schema.
Returns valid:bool, message:str

functions.name: mariqt.tests.isValidDatapath
documentation: Check whether a path leads to a proper leaf data folder (raw, processed, etc.)

functions.name: mariqt.tests.isValidEventName
documentation: Check whether an event name follows the conventions: `<project>[-<project part>]_<event-id>[-<event-id-index>][_<device acronym>]`

functions.name: mariqt.tests.isValidEquipmentID
documentation: Check whether an equipment id follows the convention: <owner>_<type>-<type index[_<subtype>[_<name>]]>

functions.name: mariqt.tests.isValidImageName
documentation:
Check whether an image filename adheres to the convention: <event>_<sensor>_<date>_<time>.<ext>.
Returns [valid:bool,msg:str]

functions.name: mariqt.tests.is_valid_ifdo_field

Check field and throw IfdoException if invalid.

functions.name: mariqt.tests.are_valid_ifdo_fields

functions.name: mariqt.tests.filesHaveUniqueName
documentation: checks if all files (with or without path) have unique file names. Returns True/False and list of duplicates

documentation: returns whether  oricd is valid my using the MOD 11-2 check digit standard

functions.name: mariqt.tests.allImageNamesValidIn

Tries to return a value from a configuration file

Recursively descending to subfolders to do the search. Returns a list of the absolute file paths to all files found.

Count the number and size of all files in a folder and its subfolders

documentation: Takes an array (a data header row) of column names and returns the index of one given column that is searched for. Returns -1 if said column name does not exist.

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileColumnNames
documentation: Returns all the column names in the table file at file_path

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileColumnIndices
documentation:
Receives a list of header field names and a list of required column names and returns the column indices of those colums.

The column names given in the optional parameter also have to be part of the required_names!

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileMaxColumnIndexRequested
documentation: Inspects a set od column indices (including multiple column requests: col1;;;col2) and returns the largest number

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileColumnValues
documentation:
Receives a line of a file, a set of column indices to extract values for and an optional column separator
Returns a dict with the column names as keys and the data values from the row as values
- convert: if True tries to convert values to float and evaluate expression (e.g. dict)

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileColumnIndicesFromFile
documentation:
Extracts column indices for requested column names from a file.

Can handle either an open file object or a path to a file as the first argument
Read from the file until a row is found that is not a comment.
The column names given in the optional parameter also have to be part of the required_names!

functions.name: mariqt.files.tabFileData
documentation:
Read all data values from a file for a given set of column names.

Returns dictionaries with column names as keys and extracted data from the file as values.
If key_col is not provided or not found in column headers the dictionaries are returned as a list.
Otherwise they are returned as a dictionary itself with the key_col value being the respecive dictionary's key. 
The column names given in the optional parameter also have to be part of the required_names!

withConverters: if True required_names is expected in format {'key': {'column-name': <column-name>, 'converter': {'name': <name>, 'params': <parans>}}

functions.name: mariqt.files.positionFromTabFileLine

functions.name: mariqt.files.attitudeFromTabFileLine

modules.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo
documentation: This class provides functionalities to create, read and adapt iFDO files

functions.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.findField
documentation: Looks for  keys ("key" or [key1,key2]) in ifdo_dict and returns its value or an empty string if key not found

functions.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDOFromFile
documentation:
Convenience function to create an iFDO object directly form an existing iFDO file. Tries to infer image files location from 'image-set-local-path'. Returns iFDO object. 
- iFDOfile: string path to load an explicit iFDO file.
- handle_prefix: string prefix of the handle server. Default: the one for Geomar.
- provenance: mariqt.provencance.Provenance object to track data provenance. Default: a new provenance object is created in the 'protocol' subfolder.
- verbose: bool whether to print out information. Processing is faster if verbose is False. Default: True.
- ignore_image_files: bool whether it's accepted that there are not images yet in 'dir'. Default: False.
- writeTmpProvFile: bool whether to write a temporary provenance file during the iFDO creation process which will be replaced by a final one in the end. Default: True
- image_broker_uuid: uuid of image broker to create image handles as https://hdl.handle.net/<handle_prefix>/<image_broker_uuid>@<image-uuid> 
- sub_folders_ignore: list of strings containing names of folders which are to be ignored while scanning dir for image data

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__getitem__
documentation:
Returns copy of checked ifdo set or item field value, also considerng default values from header.
Use keys as e.g. '<item>:<key>:...'. Can be used for header fields as well as item fields.
Item index can be leglected, in case of video a dict {<image-datatime>:<value>,...} is returned.
Raises IfdoException if item does not exist.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.getImagesPositions
documentation:
Returns images first position(s) 
@return: {'imageName': [{'lat': value, 'lon': value, 'datetime': value}]}, image-coordinate-reference-system

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.writeWorldFilesForPhotos
documentation:
Writes world files for photos if all required fields are there under the assumption that the camera was looking straight down (pitch and roll are ignored!). 
Returns a list of items for which creation failed: [[item,msg],...]

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.checkItemHash
documentation: compares file's hash with hash in iFDO and throws exception if they don't match

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.getImageDirectory
documentation: Returns the lowermost local directory containing the image files inferred from 'image-set-local-path'. If 'image-set-local-path' not provided for all image items the iFDO's parent directory is returned.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__tryGetIsDomePort
documentation:
Tries to read port type from 'image-camera-housing-viewport'[viewport-type] or 'image-flatport-parameters'/'image-domeport-parameters' 
Returns True,msg if dome port, False,msg if flat port, None,msg otherwise

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__tryGetFocalLengthInPixels
documentation:
Tries to read/determine focal length in pixels either from 'image-camera-calibration-model'['calibration-focal-length-xy-pixel'] or from exif values.
if domePort = False, flat port is assumed and a correction factor of 1.33 is applied for focal length determined from exif values
Returns either focalLength, message or [focalLengthX,focalLengthY], message. If unsuccessful focalLength = -1

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__tryDetermineFocalLenghtInPixelsFromExif_1
documentation:
try to determine focal length in pixels from exif tags Focal Length, Focal Plane X Resolution, Focal Plane Y Resolution, Focal Plane Resolution Unit.
Retruns [focalLengthPixels_x,focalLengthPixels_y], message
Retruns focalLengthPixels = -1 if not successfull

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__tryDetermineFocalLenghtInPixelsFromExif_2
documentation:
try to determine focal length in pixels from 35 mm equivalent focal length in exif tag Focal Length's add on e.g. '7.0 mm (35 mm equivalent: 38.8 mm)'.
Retruns [focalLengthPixels_x,focalLengthPixels_y], message
Retruns focalLengthPixels = -1 if not successfull

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__unitConversionFact2mm
documentation: looks for letter sequence in unit and checks if it's 'inches','m','cm','mm','um' or '' and returns respective conversion factor to mm (if there are no letters it returns 1). Otherwise return -1

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__getItemDefaultValue
documentation: returns item values (first entry in case of videos). Throws mariqt.core.IfdoException if field not found.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__getItemLatLon
documentation: returns {'lat': value, 'lon': value, 'datetime': value} of list of those

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO_Reader.__parse2LatLonDict
documentation: returns {'lat':lat,'lon':lon,'datetime':datetime}

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.__init__
documentation:
Creates an iFOD object. Requires a valid directory containing image data or/and and iFDO file and a handle prefix if it's not the Geomar one. Loads directory's iFDO file if it exists already.
- dir: mariqt.directories.Dir object pointing to a valid data type directory (raw, intermediate, processed, ...) containing the image data.
- handle_prefix: string prefix of the handle server. Default: the one for Geomar.
- provenance: mariqt.provencance.Provenance object to track data provenance. Default: a new provenance object is created in the 'protocol' subfolder.
- verbose: bool whether to print out information. Default: True.
- ignore_image_files: images files are ignored. They are not searched for in 'raw' and items values are not updated nor checked. Default = False
- writeTmpProvFile: bool whether to write a temporary provenance file during the iFDO creation process which will be replaced by a final one in the end. Default: True
- iFDOfile: string path to load an explicit iFDO file. If not provided a matching iFDO file (if it already exists) will be loaded from the 'products' subdirectory. Default: None 
- image_broker_uuid: uuid of image broker to create image handles as https://hdl.handle.net/<handle_prefix>/<image_broker_uuid>@<image-uuid> 
- create_all_type_folders: whether to create all type folders (external, intermediate, processed, ...) or only the needed ones 
- sub_folders_ignore: list of strings containing names of folders which are to be ignored while scanning dir for image data

returns copy of set or item field value, also considerng default values from header.

documentation: it can happen that an image timestamp does not contain any fields but the timestamp any more. Those are removed here.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.checkAllItemHashes
documentation:
Checks if hashes in iFDO match hashes in intermeidate hash file if the latter was changed last after the images has changed.
Otherwise or if hard==True it redetermines the actuall file's hash and compares the iFDO item's hash with that.
If hashes do not match a mariqt.core.IfdoException is risen unsless raiseException == False, then a list of lists [<file>,<exception>] is returned.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_uuid_file
documentation:
Creates in /intermediate a text file containing per image a created uuid (version 4).

The UUID is only *taken* from the metadata of the images. It does not write UUIDs to the metadata in case some files are missing it.
But, it creates a CSV file in that case that you can use together with exiftool to add the UUID to your data. Beware! this can destroy your images
if not done properly! No guarantee is given it will work. Be careful!

Use clean=False to not check those files again which are already found in intermediate uuid file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createUUIDFile
documentation: Deprecated - use create_uuid_file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_image_set_attitude
documentation:
calculates the the cameras absolute attitude and sets it to image set header. All angles are expected in degrees. 
camera2frame angles: rotation of camera coordinates (x,y,z = top, right, line of sight) with respect to vehicle coordinates (x,y,z = forward,right,down) 
in accordance with the yaw,pitch,roll rotation order convention:

1. yaw around z,
2. pitch around rotated y,
3. roll around rotated x

Rotation directions according to 'right-hand rule'.

I.e. camera2Frame angles = 0,0,0 camera is facing downward with top side towards vehicle's forward direction'

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.setImageSetAttitude
documentation: Deprecated - use set_image_set_attitude

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_image_attitude_file
documentation:
Creates in /intermediate a text file with camera attitude data for each image. All angles are expected in degrees. Use att_path_angles_in_rad if necessary. 
camera2Frame angles: rotation of camera coordinates (x,y,z = top, right, line of sight) with respect to vehicle coordinates (x,y,z = forward,right,down) 
in accordance with the yaw,pitch,roll rotation order convention:
1. yaw around z,
2. pitch around rotated y,
3. roll around rotated x

Rotation directions according to 'right-hand rule'.

I.e. camera2Frame angles = 0,0,0 camera is facing downward with top side towards vehicle's forward direction'

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createImageAttitudeFile
documentation: Deprecatde - use create_image_attitude_file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_R_camStd2utm
documentation: retrun rotation matrix R tranforming from camStd: (x,y,z = right,buttom,line of sight) to utm (x,y,z = easting,northing,up)

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.findNavDataForImage
documentation:
creates a dict (image_dts) with file name as key and a list of data elements as value. 
In case of photos the list has only a single entry, for videos it has video duration [sec] / videoSampleSeconds entries.
Returns success, image_dts, msg

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_image_navigation_file
documentation:
Creates in /intermediate a text file with 4.5D navigation data for each image, i.e. a single row per photo, video duration [sec] / videoSampleSeconds rows per video.
nav_header must be dict containing the keys 'utc','lat','lon','dep'(or 'alt'), optional: 'hgt','uncert' with the respective column headers as values 
if one of the vehicle x,y,z offsets [m] is not 0 and nav_header also contains 'yaw','pitch','roll' leverarm offsets are compensated for

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createImageNavigationFile
documentation: Deprecated - use create_image_navigation_file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_image_sha256_file
documentation:
Creates in /intermediate a text file containing per image its SHA256 hash.
If reReadAll is True all images' hashes are determined. Otherwise only for those files
which are not yet in the intermediate file containing the image hashes

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createImageSHA256File
documentation: Deprecated - use create_image_sha256_file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_start_time_file
documentation: Creates in /intermediate a text file containing per image its start time parsed from the file name

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createStartTimeFile
documentation: Deprecated - use create_start_time_file

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.createAcquisitionSettingsEXIFFile
documentation: Deprecated. Use create_acquisition_settings_exif_file() instead.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.create_acquisition_settings_exif_file
documentation: Creates in /intermediate a text file containing per image a dict of exif tags and their values parsed from the image

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.parseItemDatafromTabFileData
documentation:
parses data from columns in cols and writes info to items. Column 'image-filename' must be in file and does not need to be passed in cols. 
File must be tab separated and columns names must equal item field names

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.praseItemDataFromFile
documentation:
parses data from from file to items. header dict must be of structure: {<item-field-name>:<column-name>}
and must contain entry 'image-filename'

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.extractConstDataValues
documentation:
Removes fields that are constant for all entries from data dict and put them in header dict. 
Each entry of data must be a dict with the same set of keys.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_equipment_handle_url
documentation:
Constructs and returns handle url from eqipment id in ifdo_field['name']. Returns "" if fails. Does not check is site is up. 
handle_prefix prefix must be of form e.g. https://hdl.handle.net/20.500.12085

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_header_equipment_uri_to_handle_url
documentation:
Constructs equipment handle url from ifdo_field['name'] and write it to ifdo_field['uri'].
Params:
    override: if False set only if uri is empty or does not exist.
    ignore_offline: write to ifdo_field['uri'] even if constructed url is not reachable.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_header_equipment_uri_to_equipment_git_url
documentation:
DEPRECATED: Rather use get_equipment_handle_url() instead.
Constructs and returns equipment url from ifdo_field['name']. Returns "" if fails. Does not check is site is up.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_header_equipment_uri_to_equipment_git_url
documentation:
DEPRECATED: Rather use set_header_equipment_uri_to_handle_url() instead.
Constructs equipment url from ifdo_field['name'] and write it to ifdo_field['uri'].
Params:
    override: if False set only if uri is empty or does not exist.
    ignore_offline: write to ifdo_field['uri'] even if constructed url is not reachable.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_header_image_project_uri_to_osis_expedition_url
documentation: Returns header image-project uri as osis expedition url parsed from image-project['name'].

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_header_image_project_uri_to_osis_expedition_url
documentation:
Sets header image-project uri to osis expedition url parsed from image-project['name'].
Params:
    override: if False set only if uri is empty or does not exist.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_header_image_event_uri_to_osis_event_url
documentation:
Returns header image-event uri as osis event url parsed from image-project and image-event['name'] or 
image-event['uri'] if it refers to osis. Returns empty string if fails.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_header_image_event_uri_to_osis_event_url
documentation:
Sets header image-event uri to osis event url parsed from image-project and image-event['name'] or 
image-event['uri'] if it refers to osis.
Params:
    override: if False set only if uri is empty or does not exist.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_osis_event_url
documentation:
Returns osis event url if osis event id can be successfully retrieved from event_name.
Returns "" if not successsful.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_license_uri
documentation: Tries to guess licence url from license name and returns it. Returns "" if fails.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.get_license_uri_to_license
documentation: Returns header image-license uri guessed from license name. Returns empty string if fails.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.set_license_uri_from_license_name
documentation:
Sets header image-license uri to url guessed from image-license.
Params:
    override: if False set only if uri is empty or does not exist.

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.convertToDefaultDateTimeFormat
documentation:
Checks if all items' 'image-datetime' fields match default datetime format or a custom one defined in 'image-datetime-format'
and converts to default format. Throws exception if datetime cannot be parsed

methods.name: mariqt.sources.ifdo.iFDO.__get_int_file_prefix
documentation: depends on 'image-event' and 'image-sensor' so it can change during upate
